title: ADV 4th of July salePlatinum
wikitext:
[]: ADV 4th of July sale...Platinum...             Quote Post by Digitalex » Thu Jun 30, 2005 3:07 pm ADV has the Platinum Box + DVD 1 on sale for $16. They also have Platinum DVD 1 on sale for $12. Being the freak I am in, I bought another Platinum Box. I couldn't pass up the sale and I absolutely love the box design. [url]http://www.advfilms.com/sales.asp[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by rip3mwk » Thu Jun 30, 2005 5:57 pm God damnit! There goes $16 down the drain...

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Jun 30, 2005 7:09 pm I hate ADV. First they take almost 12 months longer to release in the UK, and now they're just rubbing it in with a sale in region 1. Why, why did the DVD cartel implement region locking?

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu Jun 30, 2005 8:15 pm If only I had $20. I'd like to eat this week.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jun 30, 2005 11:10 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: I hate ADV. First they take almost 12 months longer to release in the UK, and now they're just rubbing it in with a sale in region 1. Why, why did the DVD cartel implement region locking?

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Thu Jun 30, 2005 11:40 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: I hate ADV. First they take almost 12 months longer to release in the UK, and now they're just rubbing it in with a sale in region 1. Why, why did the DVD cartel implement region locking?

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Fri Jul 01, 2005 11:59 am You are also forgetting that in order for you to actually get what you paid for from ADV, you have to send them at least two nasty e-mails asking why in the hell haven't they delivered your order yet. :?

[]:             Quote Post by Digitalex » Fri Jul 01, 2005 1:54 pm Considering it's my first time ordering directly from them I'll have to wait and see what experience I have with them. I guess I should expect the worst huh? I've been spoiled by Amazon. Those guys have exceeded all my expectations.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jul 01, 2005 2:05 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Why, why did the DVD cartel implement region locking?

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Sat Jul 02, 2005 6:25 am those.......bastards. what do you think the chances are that they're going to do this for advfilms.co.uk ? i could play the NTSC on my computer, but i'd prefer a PAL version cos i'm fussy like that.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Jul 02, 2005 8:52 am NTSC/PAL doesn't even matter anymore. All my TV's play both just fine. I think this is because they are manufactured for worldwide distribution and instead of two models one is produced. About the only reason for region coding anymore is to restrict the market and have us all wait 12 months for DVD releases.

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Sat Jul 02, 2005 2:44 pm i've had a look on advfilms.us and apparently they only ship to canada and the usa. i was going to buy it but that had to happen. anyone know of a way round it? i doubt there is, but i'm desperate.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sat Jul 02, 2005 9:02 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: NTSC/PAL doesn't even matter anymore. All my TV's play both just fine. I think this is because they are manufactured for worldwide distribution and instead of two models one is produced. About the only reason for region coding anymore is to restrict the market and have us all wait 12 months for DVD releases.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Wed Jul 06, 2005 4:59 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: NTSC/PAL doesn't even matter anymore. All my TV's play both just fine. I think this is because they are manufactured for worldwide distribution and instead of two models one is produced.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 06, 2005 8:03 am ice reaper wrote: i've had a look on advfilms.us and apparently they only ship to canada and the usa. i was going to buy it but that had to happen. anyone know of a way round it? i doubt there is, but i'm desperate.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Jul 06, 2005 10:17 am the_seventh_child wrote: ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: NTSC/PAL doesn't even matter anymore. All my TV's play both just fine. I think this is because they are manufactured for worldwide distribution and instead of two models one is produced.

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Thu Jul 07, 2005 11:25 am Pay one of us boorish colonials a small fee to serve as a proxy. (Plus, of course, the shipping to both vis house and subsequently to yours comes out of YOUR wallet.)

[]:             Quote Post by Digitalex » Thu Jul 07, 2005 11:40 am ice reaper wrote: Pay one of us boorish colonials a small fee to serve as a proxy. (Plus, of course, the shipping to both vis house and subsequently to yours comes out of YOUR wallet.)

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Thu Jul 07, 2005 12:58 pm thanks

[]:             Quote Post by Digitalex » Sun Jul 17, 2005 5:37 pm Hunter21 wrote: You are also forgetting that in order for you to actually get what you paid for from ADV, you have to send them at least two nasty e-mails asking why in the hell haven't they delivered your order yet. :?
